title: Iteratively Removing Predicted Noise
concept: At each step, the trained network predicts the noise present, and a small amount of that predicted noise is subtracted from the current image.

exercises.prompt: When you erase a smudge on paper, do you rub it all off in one swipe or a little bit at a time?

options.text: A little bit at a time, checking as you go
You never erase, you start over

exercises.explain: Exactly! Erasing slowly, bit by bit, gives you more control. AI image generators clean up 'noisy' pictures the same careful way.

cards.title: How Does a Blurry Mess Become a Picture?
cards.body: Imagine starting with a screen full of random TV static. Somehow, an AI turns that mess into a photo of a cat. It doesn't happen in one flash — it happens step by step, like a photo slowly developing in front of your eyes!

cards.title: Iteratively Removing Predicted Noise
cards.body: This means the AI looks at a noisy image, guesses exactly what the 'noise' part looks like, and removes just a small piece of that noise. Then it looks again, guesses again, and removes a little more. It repeats this many times until a clear image appears.

cards.title: Cleaning a Foggy Window
cards.body: Think of a foggy car window. You don't wipe it clean in one motion — you wipe a little, check what you can see, wipe a little more, and check again. Each small wipe reveals more of the view outside, just like each step removes a bit more noise to reveal the image.

cards.title: Step-by-Step Reveal
cards.body: Step 1: The AI sees a mostly-static image and predicts 'this part is noise.' Step 2: It subtracts a tiny bit of that predicted noise. Step 3: It looks at the new, slightly clearer image and repeats. After 50 or so repeats, a sharp picture of a mountain appears!

cards.title: Myth: One Big Guess Does It All
cards.body: Many people think the AI predicts the final image in a single shot. Not true! It never guesses the whole picture at once. It removes noise gradually, in many small steps, because tiny careful guesses work far better than one giant risky guess.

cards.title: One Giant Jump vs. Many Small Steps
cards.body: Removing noise works best in small increments, not all at once.

left.text: Guess all the noise at once — risky, often creates a blurry or broken image

right.text: Guess and remove a little noise repeatedly — steady, careful, produces a clear image

exercises.prompt: What does the AI do at each step of the denoising process?

options.text: Predicts the noise and removes a small amount of it
Deletes the entire image and starts over
Adds more random noise to the image
Copies a picture from the internet

exercises.explain: Correct! At each step, the network predicts the noise present and subtracts just a small amount, slowly revealing the image.

exercises.prompt: True or False: The AI removes all the noise from an image in just one single step.
exercises.explain: False! The AI removes noise gradually, over many small steps, not all at once.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the idea.
exercises.sentence: At each step, the network predicts the ___ and removes a small amount of it.
exercises.explain: The network's job at every step is to predict the noise present in the image, then subtract a small piece of it.

exercises.prompt: Which description best matches how AI image generation actually works?

options.text: The AI predicts a tiny bit of noise, removes it, and repeats many times until the image is clear
The AI instantly knows the full final image and draws it in one step
The AI randomly guesses colors until something looks right by luck

exercises.explain: Right! Real AI image generators work through many small, careful steps of removing predicted noise, not one instant guess or random luck.

exercises.prompt: Boss Challenge: An AI is turning static into a picture of a dog. How does it actually do this?

options.text: It predicts a little noise, subtracts a small amount, then repeats this process many times
It draws the dog perfectly on the very first try
It searches the internet for a real dog photo and copies it exactly
It removes all the noise in one massive subtraction

exercises.explain: That's it! The AI works iteratively — predicting and removing a little noise at a time, step after step, until the clear dog image emerges.
